questions.question: According to the lesson, how long does the peak of an intense emotion typically last?

options.text: It varies too much to estimate

questions.explanation: The peak of any emotion typically lasts only 60 to 90 seconds. Emotions do not persist on their own — they are kept going by the thoughts and reactions we add to them. Knowing this makes it much easier to choose to ride the wave rather than fight it.

questions.question: Why does fighting or suppressing an emotion often make it stronger?

options.text: It signals to your brain that the emotion is too powerful to handle
It adds layers of shame, frustration, or fear on top of the original feeling, fueling a bigger wave
It causes your body to release more adrenaline as a defense
It has no effect — emotions always stay the same regardless of how we respond

questions.explanation: Fighting an emotion adds shame and frustration on top of it. Suppressing it stores the energy in your body. Catastrophizing layers fear on top of the original feeling. Each of these reactions generates new fuel, making the wave larger rather than letting it recede naturally.

questions.question: What is the first step in the four-step 'surfing' process described in the lesson?

options.text: Let Go of the Story — detach from the thoughts behind the emotion
Breathe Through It — use slow paced breathing as an anchor
Notice and Name — acknowledge the feeling without judgment
Stay in the Present — observe the sensation with curiosity

questions.explanation: The first step is to Notice and Name the emotion without judgment — simply saying to yourself 'This is anxiety' or 'I am feeling a wave of anger.' Research shows that labeling an emotion reduces its intensity in the brain and helps you separate from it rather than be consumed by it.

questions.question: How is Riding the Wave different from ignoring your feelings?

options.text: They are essentially the same — both involve not reacting to emotions
Riding the Wave means distracting yourself until the feeling fades
Riding the Wave means paying close attention to the emotion while choosing not to react impulsively — letting it speak without letting it drive
Riding the Wave means resolving the situation causing the emotion immediately

questions.explanation: Riding the Wave is the opposite of ignoring. You are paying close, respectful attention to the emotion — observing where it is in your body, noticing its qualities. You are simply choosing not to react impulsively to it. You are letting it speak without letting it drive.

questions.question: Recall a recent time when you experienced a strong emotion. Looking back, can you identify the wave pattern — the building, the peak, and the receding? What did you do during the peak, and what might you try differently using the surfing steps next time?
questions.explanation: Recognizing the wave pattern in hindsight is the first step to catching it in real time. Most people are surprised to discover how brief the true peak actually was. Each time you look back and see the wave, it becomes easier to trust that it will pass when you are in the middle of it.

title: Riding the Wave of Emotion - Quiz
